rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY NANG TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/29/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 07/05/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be verified by the petitioner. 4) Paragraph 7 of the petition: i) An allegation must also be made as to whether any heir HAS PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). ii) The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 5) Paragraph 10: Was notice of each proposed action given to the probate referee who requested special notice? 6) Paragraph 15: No summary of account was attached to the petition. 7) Paragraph 18: No Attachment 1 (schedule of proposed distribution) was attached to the petition. 8) Paragraph 19: No assignment has been filed as to Jacob Swaim's interest. 9) Paragraph 20 & Schedule A: If the $2,899.78 payment is a partial or full refund of selling expenses deducted from the proceeds through escrow, the statutory fee base receipts must be reduced by $2,899.78. 10) Paragraph 24: No declaration itemizing the costs sought to be reimbursed has been filed. 11) No schedule of disbursements was attached to the petition. 12) No schedule of property on hand at close of account was attached to the petition. 13) Financial account statements must be filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of original statements required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. Note: An appearance may be required on 10/29/2024 for the Court to consider whether this petition should be taken off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed by 10/22/2024.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/24. (No earlier date available.) (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice. (Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.]) Deadline is 10/21/24 to provide the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If a petition for final distribution is filed by 10/16/24, the time the personal representative claims in this petition needed to close the estate, this petition will be taken off calendar on 10/30/24.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN WILLIAM BARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. An AMENDED petition was filed. An amending petition requires the same notice, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).] Both the mailed and published notice must clearly show that an AMENDED PETITION has been filed. 2. Item 8 of the amended petition does not show the RELATIONSHIP to the decedent of each person listed. 3. Items 1 and 9 of the amended petition are also incomplete. Deadline is 10/21/24 to provide courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of any papers filed to address these issues. Aside from the proof of service and proof of publication of the AMENDED petition, the other items may be addressed in a SUPPLEMENT to the amended petition. [See CRC 7.3 for definitions.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID CLEMENTS JONSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 7/7/21. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required; however, although Petitioner clarified the difference in the spelling of Decedent's last name from his father's, there was no explanation given for Decedent's predeceased spouse's last name not being that of either the spouse's biological father's or his stepfather's -- or even his mother's. Status of closing the estate will be set for 9/16/25. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 9/16/25, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION

Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before that status date. Appearance will be required on 9/16/25 if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALICIA P. ALBAHAE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UTD 07/16/2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/5/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Please file a copy of the vesting deed to show how title is currently held. (2) Trustorâs heirs at law must be identified and must receive personal service of notice. A beneficiary of the trust receives mailed notice with a copy of the petition, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/25/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
